questions.question: Describe the main difference between a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) and a Nondeterministic Finite Automaton (NFA) in terms of how they handle the input string. (10 marks)

Given a DFA M = (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F), describe how you would find all strings in the input alphabet Σ that are accepted by M. Provide an example with Σ = {0, 1}, Q = {q0, q1}, δ = {(q0, 0) -> q0, (q0, 1) -> q1, (q1, 0) -> q1, (q1, 1) -> q0}, q0 = q0, F = {q1}. (15 marks)

Consider a DFA M = (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F) with ε-transitions, where δ contains ε-transitions. Describe how the introduction of ε-transitions affects the behavior of the DFA, and provide an example to illustrate your answer. (12 marks)
